name: Chat bubble
goal: Render one chat message with role-driven coloring, avatar placement, header metadata (author/model/timestamp), and a tokens-aware footer.
description: Single LLM-chat message bubble. The role attribute drives color tinting (assistant=blue, user=purple, system=neutral, tool=amber) and avatar placement (user-aligned right; everyone else left). Header surfaces author + model + timestamp; footer surfaces a slotted footer + an optional tokens count. Pure presentation; emits no events.

props.name: role
props.description: Chat role; drives color, alignment, and avatar placement.

props.name: author
props.description: Display name shown in the header.

props.name: timestamp
props.description: ISO-8601 or pre-formatted timestamp string; rendered verbatim.

props.name: model
props.description: Optional model identifier shown next to the author.

props.name: tokens
props.description: Optional token count; renders as a footer chip when set.

props.name: followUp
props.description: Opt-in flag set externally (typically by ce-message-group) to mark this bubble as a follow-up in a same-role cluster. Hides the avatar column, suppresses the head row, and tightens the outer top margin.

slots.name: avatar
slots.description: Small avatar element placed beside the content (opposite the role's alignment).

slots.name: footer
slots.description: Extra footer content; rendered before the auto-generated tokens chip.

cssVariables.name: --ce-space-3
cssVariables.description: Outer margin + content padding y + grid gap.

sideEffects.description: Sets aria-roledescription=message on the host so the role attribute (which carries the chat role) doesn't shadow conventional ARIA semantics.
sideEffects.reason: Reconciles the role= chat-role API with screen-reader expectations.

a11y.notes: aria-label is set from the author attribute when present; aria-roledescription=message identifies the bubble to assistive tech without requiring a literal ARIA role (the role attribute is taken by the chat-role API).
